content: Hi guys. I just got back to the game after the first GDoll banner. This is my account's characters and Wod. Should I play another account? And start from the beginning.
there's barely any reason to reroll in this game, since account level is really important for progression. Also, you already have good units like Mouchette and Saya.
Check Cheri's guide and see what comps you can make with what you want to play

I think Im gonna keep this acc, tks guys again

Ever since they announced Saya's Collab, I started to save up to pull her frequently. 

I am on the path to pull more of her card, but in the meantime, I wanted some suggestions or tips for maximizing her corrosion dmg as well as generating aliemus properly for her. My usual team comp is with faint, thais, the choice between doresain and helot: catena, and finally saya. I feel like I could switch thais out but I am not sure who to replace her with. Thoughts?

I'll admit that I'm not knowledgeable with GHelot comps (or most teams with a caro main dps, for that matter), but from what I've read from other players that do, GHelot and Saya are anti-synergistic (which makes sense, since GHelot wants to retain cards and Saya wants to cycle through her corrosion card). Same deal with Doresain, since, apparently, corrosion damage tends to steal his kills when it comes to corpse generation (there's even a petition from some players to change that, since otherwise they could pair well together). Meanwhile, I've heard good things about pairing Saya with Sorel (propagation fiesta goes brrr and, overall, Saya likes exalt focused dps).

If you're deep in the caro trenches, then your main source of aliemus can perfectly be your embryos (plus Saya doesn't need to exalt that often anyway). Honestly, Thais is the last member of your Saya team that I would consider swapping out. If you feel that your embryo generation is lacking, Leigh comes to mind instead of Faint, but honestly, Saya/Thais should be plenty.

Ive been carrying faint since i started playing morimens, she is my backbone for my caro teams if i get a slow start. The counter, strength drawn, healing and death res stacking just does wonders.

Funny how you mentioned sorel as I had swapped out my dps for her and it is doing wonders. Currently in chapter 8 atm and i stacked 58k corrosion against ||tawil|| and went to town with sorel by duplicating her multi hit attacks.

I think i just might need to work on not performing the red exalt on saya. I honestly didn't realize how much aliemus you need to get saya's ready, let alone requiring double of the base to over exalt. 

I just wasnt quite sure if i was approaching saya correctly as Im basically using three supports and one dps.

I do make it work with Ghelot sometimes though, since you start with a free card pick from her deck and it doesn't get rotated out from saya, I usually hold it off until I am prepared to burst dps, but again, the rotation can be slow like that.
3 supports and 1 dps is pretty standard tbh, so don't worry about it. Cheri's guide has a section on teambuilding, I suggest you check it out if you haven't done so already

There has been a video I’ve seen on YouTube should I send the link here ?
This is my roster for now, should I consider pulling for kath e3 and pls give me some advice on what do I need to pull to complete my team

I'll assume with team you mean story, in that case you don't need to touch Kath, since you already have an e2 Mouchette. Mouchette/Saya/Aigis/Faint should be survivable enough to let Mouchette+Saya burst most of arc1. You can also try swapping her teammates depending on what the boss requires of you at any time (Helot for tons more burst, Leigh for more healing, to name two).
If you want to play Kath for sure, Kath/Arachne/Horla/Clementine is one of her best teams. Tinct also works really well instead of Arachne.
Sidenote, use Soul Rewind Cores on arc2 units only, and only after you know for sure why you're picking them. It is a pretty precious resource.
also, check Cheri's guide, she has plenty of information on team building and recommendations depending on what awakeners you want to bring to the fray

You clearly have a bias towards Ultra and Jenkin, so I would suggest checking Cheri's recommendations with Jenkin as a main DPS. If it came down to me, I would first try Jenkin/Clementine/Horla and a last pick between Castor or Murphy if you feel defense is lacking, or Pickman, Saya or Tinct if you feel you need more burst

Which characters would you reccomend I try to get? Aside from the obvious Thais and Horla whenever I get the chance
I was thinking getting Mouch from the rotating banner (I have enough to hard pity), but I'm open to suggestions (specially since I got Pollux and Ryker the other day)

I mean I saw the tier list, the rotating banner guide and Cheri's handbook, but I wasn't sure where to start

If you're just starting out, people will usually recommend to make a team around one dps for story. Mouchette and Vortice are the two everyone will recommend since they require low enlightments for their breakpoints (Vortice at e1, Mouchette at e2) and they get even better as you progress further, but at the end of the day it's a matter of preference. Just make sure that whatever dps you pick is one that you'll enjoy playing and that you want to teambuild around.

Further recommendations will come from what other units synergize well with your unit(s) of choice, although some supports are so versatile they fit in a myriad of setups (think Thais, Arachne, Horla, Clementine, Aigis).

Also, keep in mind that you need, at least, 19 units for dtide. Just make sure to spread your standard pulls amongst the different realms for a bit to get a bunch of awakeners before committing to getting Blade of the Titan +12 and/or focusing on one realm.

If you're aiming for Mouchette, Mouchette/Thais/Helot/Aigis is a fairly common team, and you can tinker with the setup as the boss in front of you demands.
Mouchette also works really well with Saya afaik
I see, tyvm ❤️

also, do you think it'd be a good idea to select Thais, Horla and 2 others on Moonless Guide? Or better to wait for their rerun? I also considered GMurphy bc apparently both the wheel and the character are good, but then I probably wouldn't have enough for the Mouch/Vortice banner
I've seen some mixed opinions about moonless guide

The issue with Moonless is that it is limited to arc 1 units and there are no guarantees, and very few units are worth the hassle. Personally, I like it if you're lacking all 4 selected characters and/or their breakpoints, but only after securing a main dps. Also, keep in mind that arc 1 units can spook you in the standard banners, albeit very rarely.
Yeah, Ryker spooked me on the standard banner ||I would've prefer for Thais to spook me on all my Caro pulls, but beggars can't be choosers I guess||

GMurphy is a nice support at lower investments, but the team everybody recommends her with as a dps is very expensive, so I would not encourage hard pushing for her, even more so with the current daily rotation.
Her wheel is quite good though, I would fish a copy if you have the resources to spare
Good thing about Ryker is that he does his job (card tutoring/drawing) at e0 and is relatively versatile, so it's not a bad spook
Yeah, from what I read he kinda works in most teams

It is
Maybe I could try like 30 pulls on GMurphy, and even if I don't get her or her wheel, it won't be wasted

how do you suppose the gameplay is like? How would I do dps if I were to use either pickman or salvador? Sorry for asking about it im sure its obvious but I'm not super familiar with a lot of characters or how they play with each other
uhhh idk about pickman as i don't have him but with salvador , it would include using pursuits with vortice with her posse abd using salvadors 2 cost aoe on normal stages
on more tanky enemy you would build up vortice exalt, use vortice rouse, use the charge card which i think is the 3 cost until she reaches high crit chance, and then popping vortice exalt
and no worries about it man, morimens is pretty complicated in the beginning

Right off the bat, I see two routes:

- Get Vortice to e1 and pair her with Murphy, Celeste or Sanga, and Tawil (hopefully at e2 at some point). The idea is to use Vortice's posse every turn to deal health % dmg from getting skey from Tawil, and to turtle through Murphy and Celeste/Sanga.

- Sanga/Saya/Thais/Salvador. The idea is to apply corrosion with Saya and to explode it in one go with Sanga's exalt. Thais provides embryos, vuln/weak and aliemus (among other things). Salvador helps with getting more armor for Sanga's exalt and hp for the forge (afaik, I'm not super experienced with Salv). Sanga would like OE at some point if going this route. Aigis (or even Myriam) could also work here as a support.

There are, for sure, more alternatives (something something kath e3, something something caecus counter team) but those two are the first ones that came to my mind. Check them out, ask around further if needed and see which one you might prefer!
ill try all those options. I just need some time to build the characters but thats fine. thanks yall! I'm still open to more suggestions if anyone else has any ideas too

First off: Faded Legacy = Arc 1, Astral Reign = Arc 2

1. If you haven’t, use the moonless guide and select Thais + Horla and 2 other limiteds from astral reign. Thais and Horla are some of the best supports in the game. After that, it’s generally recommended to pull from either specific or the astral reign banner since arc 2 characters are generally all good. Do note, the characters you get are random, so if you want more control, do the 24h banners since there are some rlly strong characters coming up (do note tho that at it’s worse, it can take a 180 pulls for a specific character).
2. You can use it if you want since your account is so new; at this point, your main priority should be **getting** a bunch of new characters 
3. Pure cores are meant to be spent to get units. D-Zone, one of the main end game content, requires 5 teams of unique characters. At a certain point, some pros recommend switching to the wheel “Blade of the Titan”, but you are so early your main goal is to just pull as many new characters as possible
4. The best silver prime value is the monthly silver which gives 6000. The next highest value is 1980 silver pre order packages that come every update. The curriculum is also very high value since it can give good wheels and tons of materials for a new account. However, I wouldn’t recommend buying the curriculum rn since it’s about to end in a few days
Ok, that’s probably information overload. If you have any questions feel free to ask. Cheri’s guide is also a really good introduction for beginners too

Thanks a lot for the suggestions, very helpful!
I checked trhe guide and I'm following it for the moment
Thanks a lot again!
but kath dont play poison, no?
gdoll isnt there for poison, she's still a great support
has vuln/weakness, dmg amp and her exalt gives aliemus to others and dmg amp
karen is there solely as an exalt bot
cause some turns you need the exalt for more arith
thais is just good everywhere, top tier support and can dupe cards with her exalt + give aliemus and embryos
note that if u wanna play kath comfortably, you wanna have e3 kath otherwise her playstyle is a bit clunky
and always rouse kath first

Arachne is one of her best teammates (besides horla and clementine, which you don't have). Kath wants str sources, dmg amplification and/or crit buffs from her teammates (and weak/vuln ofc), and Arachne is one of the biggest sources of dmg amp currently. Tinct is also quite good, since her crit buffs are quite beefy (particularly at e3, her 3 cost can provide 45% crit chance and crit dmg) and her exalt discounts the cost of Kath's cards, which she really likes.
Otherwise, Leigh is another really good option, since she brings quite a bit of str and healing. Thais' dupe mechanic also works really well with Kath.
GRamona and Ryker are also known to work well with her, since Kath really wants to use her posse every turn and to draw her cards as much as possible. I don't have enough experience with those two to fully endorse it though, I just know they're also used.

Try to get her e2 then use a prototype horizon on her from the store to get e3 if you can
